design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore islands, defeat enemies by clubbing (not jumping), collect bones as currency, purchase upgrades from shops to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (bones for upgrades) balanced against single-hit death vulnerability; long campaign with punishing checkpoint design
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Prehistoric caveman survival and island exploration with progression through accumulated upgrades
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Inverts typical platformer combat—clubbing enemies instead of jumping on them. Refuses to let players restart from checkpoints after game over; demands full campaign completion or restart. Emphasizes resource management (bones) and shop-based progression over reflexive jumping

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives; no checkpoint restart after all lives exhausted—must restart entire campaign
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoints exist within levels but are negated if player loses all lives; no mid-game continues
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden configuration flags allow infinite lives, infinite bones, and stage select (debug features), suggesting developer acknowledgment of difficulty frustration. Standard play offers no difficulty settings

progression.upgrade_system: Shop-based purchases using collected bones currency. Upgrades include spells, ability enhancements, and items. Stones provide projectile weapon via club (up to 3 stones per shot)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through four sequential islands (Paradise Island, Monster Island, Terror Island, Chaos Island), each with multiple levels. Bone collection gates access to stronger equipment
replayability_hooks: Multiple islands with environmental variety (pitfalls, traps, lava, water hazards)
Varied shop items and upgrade combinations
Long campaign incentivizes replay for skill mastery given harsh checkpoint system

world_building_techniques: Island-based setting names (Paradise, Monster, Terror, Chaos) establish escalating danger
Prehistoric theme with caveman protagonist and dinosaur enemies
Implied survival narrative: character is hungry, seeking dinner across islands

danger_signals: Island naming progression suggests escalating threat (Paradise → Monster → Terror → Chaos)
Environmental hazards: pitfalls, traps, lava, water (caveman cannot swim)
Single-hit death mechanic (unless protected by stones) creates constant tension
environmental_storytelling: Four distinct islands with thematic progression toward chaos/danger
Shop locations suggest primitive trade/civilization hubs within each island
Prehistoric setting with dinosaur enemies implies time-displaced world
